prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/vapor/projects/realm/re-arc-2/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `1be83260`.
codex_response:
Rule: crop to the nonzero bounding box, split it on full-zero separator rows and columns to recover the repeated tile lattice, read the marked tile’s odd/odd positions as the macro color map, then rebuild the lattice with the base color filling separators and internal holes. I used the `arc2_opus46_summary.json` hint and discarded the `arc2_sonnet45_summary.jsonl` hint because Sonnet misframed the task as per-row “marker combinations” instead of one marked tile encoding the full output map.

The generator matches the family by explicitly sampling a small macro lattice (`2x2` through `3x3`), building the self-similar base motif with odd/odd hole slots, placing one marked tile in a zero-padded repeated array, and rendering output from that same latent map. No new DSL primitive was added. Verification passed on all official examples (`4/4`), all generated examples (`1000/1000`), and the preview sheet looked in-family.
